vocabulary_items.raw_line: well bred person: umuntu yarezwe neza
the kinsmen: abantu bo mu muryango umwe
step brother: umwana wa so wanyu
step sister: umwana wa so wanyu
sport shoes: ibirato vyo kwinonora imitsi
day before yesterday: hirya y'ejo hahise
day after tomorrow: hirya y'ejo hazoza
at the sun rise: izuba riserutse
at the sunset: ku kirenga zuba
skin disease: indwara yo ku rukoba
to be in good health: kugira amagara meza
television engineer: umufundi wa za mbonehakure
first vice-president: icegera ca mbere c'umukuru w'igihugu
second vice-president: icegera ca kabiri c'umukuru w'igihugu
junior minister: umunyamabanga mukuru wa leta
secretary general: umunyamabanga mukuru wa leta
the no go area: ahantu hatagira umutekano
an amphibian: igikoko kiba mu mazi
a reptile: igikoko gikwega inda hasi
the rear-view mirror: ikiyo co ku ruhande
the number plate: inimero ndanga muduga
to turn left: gukatira i bubamfu
to turn right: gukatira i buryo
to hit a tree: kugonga igiti
a bike taxi-man: uwunguruza abantu n'ikinga

phrases.translation_en: Where are you in a hurry to?
I'm in a hurry to church
Could you tell me your name?
What do you do in your life?
I mustn't grumble. Sorry to trouble you. What's your name?

phrases.phrase_kirundi: Nitwa Jen Akimana, nawe witwa nde?
phrases.translation_en: I'm Jen Akimana, and how are you called?

phrases.phrase_kirundi: None Mark, uvuye he kandi ugiye he?
phrases.translation_en: So Mark, where do you come from and where to?

phrases.phrase_kirundi: Mvuye i muhira ngiye mu gisagara. Wavukiye he kandi ryari?
phrases.translation_en: I come from home and I'm going to town. Anyway, where and when were you born?

phrases.phrase_kirundi: Navutse i Gitega, mu 1988. Nawe ufise imyaka ingahe?
phrases.translation_en: I was born in Gitega, 1988. And how old are you?

phrases.phrase_kirundi: Mba i Ruhagarika hafi y'ishure Iwacu Kazoza
phrases.translation_en: I live at Ruhagarika near Iwacu Kazoza School
Look at the time Mark, goodbye!
Can I have a soap please?
Could I have a cup of tea please?
Could you give your phone number please?
Can you show me the way to Buyenzi?

phrases.phrase_kirundi: Murantunga, mwomenyera Umuyobozi ari mu biro?
phrases.translation_en: Excuse me, do you know if the headmaster is in the office?

phrases.phrase_kirundi: Murantunga, iyi yoba iri yo nzira ija ku biro vya posita?
phrases.translation_en: Excuse me, is this the right way for the Post Office?
It's only something small, but I hope you'll like it.
Thank you so much! It's wonderful.
I don't like sport at all.

phrases.phrase_kirundi: Woba ufise akanya mu ndwi iza?

phrases.translation_en: I feel a bit under the weather.
I think I'm going down with a cold.
I have a touch of flu.
Maybe you should go home and get some rest.

phrases.phrase_kirundi: Mbe uzoba ufise akanya k'umunsi wa kane uza?

phrases.translation_en: That's very kind of you, thanks.

phrases.phrase_kirundi: Vyari vyiza ariko mfise ibindi nzobandiko ndakora kuwa gatandatu.
phrases.translation_en: That's very kind of you, but I'm doing something else on Saturday.
Excuse me, I think you've given me the wrong change.

phrases.phrase_kirundi: Murantunga gato, hariho akagorane ku nsusuruzi mu cumba canje
phrases.translation_en: Excuse me, but there's a problem with the heating in my room.
Shall I open the window for you?
Could you open the door for me, please?
Can I use your computer, please?
Could I borrow some money from you, please?
The news came as a complete shock.
